name: ETH 4h Exhaustion Reacceptance Rotation v1
edge_mechanism: a failed directional extension reaccepts prior value and rotates back toward medium balance in a pure chop environment
thesis: The cycle wants mean reversion from exhaustion only when structure reaccepts prior value. ETH 4h is the cleanest fit because it is pure chop and should produce the clearest failed-extension behavior in the basket. The edge is price losing acceptance away from value and rotating back once value is reclaimed.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h reacceptance events remain too sparse, or if reclaiming value still fails to produce enough move back toward balance after costs, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from prior value, cannot sustain the excursion, reclaims value, and rotates toward medium balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a 100% chop regime, failed directional extensions should outnumber sustained breakouts. Reacceptance after the failed excursion is a direct structural expression of that reality.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is the cleanest pure-chop lane in the basket and should preserve the mechanism better than DOGE or TAO.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may happen inside a genuine directional move, causing repeated fades of real continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets work but second targets underperform, reduce target ambition and keep the trigger unchanged before adding filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Frequent first-target captures, a fair number of second-target hits, low reliance on large runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h shows non-sparse reacceptance participation and at least one cross-lane preserves the same mechanism.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or repeated reacceptance failures before first partial.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low relative to recent failed families because the mechanism is stretch plus reacceptance

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit pure-chop lane for exhaustion reacceptance mean reversion.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reacceptance rotations and first-target behavior clears costs.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h remains too sparse or too shallow to monetize.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate-to-high participation with many modest balance recoveries.

test_lanes.why_included: Ensures the cycle primary lane is still represented even if ETH is the better fit for this thesis.
test_lanes.continue_if: DOGE 4h preserves failed-extension reacceptance with enough density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h is too erratic and destroys the mechanism.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Higher amplitude, noisier reacceptance than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same mechanism survives a lane with more trend contamination.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h still shows failed excursions snapping back toward value with enough structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the mechanism is too anti-trend there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, larger balance rotations than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on touch of EMA_55 or 4 bars
